findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_memories USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[dim])
findings.notes: Core sqlite-vec virtual table for KNN search. Turso lacks loadExtension and vec0 — blocks vector search entirely.
LoadExtension API for sqlite-vec. Turso (Rust) has no loadExtension — must reimplement vector search natively or via external service.

findings.signature: database.pragma('journal_mode = WAL') + 7 other pragmas in initialize_db
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 .pragma() API used for WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys, cache_size, mmap_size, synchronous, wal_autocheckpoint, temp_store. Turso has different pragma interface.
Multi-database ATTACH/DETACH for per-embedder shard isolation. Turso libSQL has different attach semantics and may not support cross-file attach.
sqlite-vec distance function for KNN queries. Must be reimplemented if Turso lacks vec extension support.
Per-dimension blob tables (e.g. vec_768) for fallback storage. Naming convention must be preserved during migration.

findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
findings.notes: FTS5 virtual table for BM25 full-text search. Turso lacks FTS5 — must use external search engine or Turso's native text search.
FTS5 bm25() ranking function with per-column weights. No equivalent in Turso — requires external BM25 implementation.

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON memory_index ... INSERT INTO memory_fts
findings.notes: FTS5 sync triggers for insert/update/delete. Turso triggers may have different behavior; FTS5 itself must be replaced first.
Schema version tracking with incremental migrations. Migration logic itself is portable but each migration's SQL must be validated against Turso.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE vec_metadata (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL)
findings.notes: Metadata table storing embedding dim/provider/model per shard. Schema is portable but lives in attached shard database.

findings.signature: Legacy profile DB → canonical + vector shard split with ATTACH DATABASE
findings.notes: Migration uses ATTACH DATABASE, vec0 virtual table recreation, and VACUUM. All three need Turso-compatible equivalents.
Used to inspect attached databases for shard management. Turso's pragma interface differs from SQLite.

findings.signature: CREATE TEMP VIEW vec_memories AS SELECT rowid, embedding FROM active_vec.vec_memories
findings.notes: Temp views and INSTEAD OF triggers for legacy compatibility aliases. Turso temp object support may differ.
Integrity check pragma used for corruption detection. Turso has different integrity verification mechanisms.

findings.signature: Staging shard → atomic rename over active shard per reindex job
findings.notes: Reindex writes to staging shard then atomically renames. Uses vec0 virtual table creation inside shard. Migration must preserve atomic swap semantics.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1), version INTEGER NOT NULL)
findings.notes: Schema version table with single-row CHECK constraint. Portable to Turso but CHECK(id=1) pattern should be validated.
Explicit WAL TRUNCATE checkpoint before close for corruption prevention. Turso has different WAL/checkpoint semantics.

findings.signature: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON memory_index(spec_folder, COALESCE(...), ...) WHERE importance_tier NOT IN ('constitutional','deprecated')
findings.notes: Partial unique index with COALESCE expressions and WHERE predicate. Turso should support this but needs validation.
